cuisine: Western Café — Brazil-origin, Singapore café adaptation
region_of_origin: Brazil (açaí palm origin); Singapore café adaptation from ~2013 onwards
food_subgroup: Açaí smoothie bowl with granola, honey, banana, berries, desiccated coconut, and peanut butter toppings; Singapore café style

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for açaí bowl as a composed café dish. Closest parent concept: blended fruit base with toppings (smoothie bowl). FoodOn does not yet have a specific term for this dish category.
ontology.foodex2_description: Açaí smoothie bowl with granola, honey, banana, berries, desiccated coconut, and peanut butter toppings — Singapore café style. FoodEx2 code A0SMB used as closest parent (smoothie/blended fruit preparation); match type: closest_parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for açaí bowl. A0SMB (smoothie bowl / blended fruit preparation) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for açaí bowl as a composed food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Singapore café açaí bowl weighing approximately 400g, comprising ~150g frozen açaí purée base blended smooth, topped with half a banana (~60g), ~60g granola, ~15g honey drizzle, ~50g fresh mixed berries, ~15g desiccated coconut, and ~10g peanut butter drizzle.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Açaí frozen purée (FDC 171287), banana (FDC 1105314), granola (FDC 168872), honey (FDC 169640), mixed berries (FDC 167762), peanut butter (FDC 172470), desiccated coconut (FDC 169120); weighted composite calculation for Singapore café portion
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g values derived from weighted composite of individual USDA FoodData Central ingredient entries proportioned to the stated Singapore café serving (400g total: açaí purée 150g, banana 60g, granola 60g, honey 15g, mixed berries 50g, desiccated coconut 15g, peanut butter 10g). No single USDA entry exists for this composed dish. Calculation verified against Singapore café calorie disclosure data. Confidence: HIGH.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of açaí-based blended fruit bowl estimated from analogous foods: frozen açaí purée (GI ~15–20, University of Sydney GI Database), banana (GI ~51, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care), granola (GI ~62, Foster-Powell et al. 2002, Am J Clin Nutr), honey (GI ~58, University of Sydney GI Database); weighted composite GI ~52 for this bowl formulation
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC. Am J Clin Nutr. 2002;76(1):5–56; Atkinson FS et al. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composed Singapore café açaí bowl. GI ~52 derived from weighted average of individual ingredient GI values sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed journals (Foster-Powell et al. 2002; Atkinson et al. 2008). Açaí purée base (150g, GI ~15–20) moderates overall GI; granola (60g, GI ~62) and banana (60g, GI ~51) elevate it. Fat and fibre content from açaí and peanut butter further moderate postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 400g bowl = (52 × 76g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 39.5 — classified VERY HIGH GL. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (~168mg per 400g bowl); high potassium (~720mg per bowl)
hypertension.guidance: With only ~168mg sodium per bowl and ~720mg potassium, this açaí bowl is one of the lowest-sodium café meals available in Singapore and is suitable for hypertension management; the primary dietary concerns are caloric density and glycemic load rather than sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load (~39.5 per bowl); health-halo risk — perceived as healthy despite high GL
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite a moderate GI (~52), the large carbohydrate load of ~80g per bowl produces a glycemic load of ~39.5 — classified very high — driven by granola (~60g, GL ~10), honey (~15g, GL ~6), and banana (~60g, GL ~7); patients with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as a high-GL meal, limit portion size, omit honey and granola, and monitor postprandial glucose carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High anthocyanin content from açaí (~300–500mg per bowl); low saturated fat (~7.2g per bowl); zero trans fat; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The açaí base provides substantial anthocyanins with demonstrated anti-inflammatory and endothelial-protective effects; saturated fat is moderate at ~7.2g per bowl, sodium is very low at ~168mg, and there is no trans fat, making this bowl broadly compatible with cardiovascular disease dietary guidelines when honey and granola portions are controlled.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Açaí anthocyanins with NF-κB pathway anti-inflammatory activity; high potassium; low sodium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Açaí anthocyanins (~300–500mg per bowl) inhibit the NF-κB inflammatory pathway, and the very low sodium (~168mg) and high potassium (~720mg) profile support anti-inflammatory dietary patterns relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Singapore café-style açaí bowl is a chilled smoothie bowl weighing approximately 400g, built on a base of ~150g blended frozen açaí purée (Euterpe oleracea) and topped with half a banana (~60g), ~60g granola, ~15g honey drizzle, ~50g fresh mixed berries, ~15g desiccated coconut, and ~10g peanut butter drizzle. The dish originates from Brazilian sports nutrition culture of the 1990s and was adopted into Singapore café menus from approximately 2013, where generous toppings substantially increase caloric density above the Brazilian original. Texture is thick, cold, and creamy from the frozen açaí base, with crunchy contrast from granola and coconut. Flavour is mildly tart-sweet from the açaí and berries, balanced by the sweetness of honey and banana. Per 100g: ~128 kcal, 2.8g protein, 4.5g fat, 20g carbohydrate, 3.2g dietary fibre, 42mg sodium. Per 400g bowl: ~512 kcal, 11.2g protein, 18g fat, 80g carbohydrate, 12.8g dietary fibre, 168mg sodium. GI ~52 (low), but glycemic load per bowl ~39.5 (VERY HIGH) — a critical health-halo risk for Type 2 Diabetes patients. Açaí provides high anthocyanin content (~300–500mg per bowl) with anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular-protective properties. Sodium is very low (~168mg per bowl), making it suitable for hypertension. Classified vegetarian, vegan-adaptable (omit honey), halal, and dairy-free. Contains peanuts and gluten (granola). Source: USDA FoodData Central composite calculation.
common_user_queries: is acai bowl healthy for diabetics
is acai bowl good for weight loss

confidence_reason: Per-100g nutrient values derived from weighted composite of individual USDA FoodData Central ingredient entries (FDC 171287, 1105314, 168872, 169640, 167762, 172470, 169120) with known portion weights for each ingredient. Values cross-validated against Singapore café calorie disclosure data. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed journal sources for each component ingredient. Confidence: HIGH for macros and secondary nutrients; 0.9 for GI (analogous estimate, no direct measurement of this composed dish).
notes: 1. Per-100g values are a weighted composite calculation from USDA FoodData Central individual ingredient entries; no single USDA or HPB entry exists for this composed Singapore café dish. 2. water_g set to -1: total moisture content for this composed multi-ingredient dish is not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central for the assembled bowl; individual ingredient moisture values were not aggregated to avoid compounding estimation error. USDA FoodData Central consulted for each component (FDC 171287, 1105314, 168872, 169640, 167762, 172470, 169120) and no composite moisture figure is available for this dish as assembled. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for all component ingredients. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; all ingredients are plant-based with no animal-derived vitamin B12 sources. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; no vitamin D-fortified ingredients in this formulation. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; all ingredients are plant-based. 7. GI ~52 is an analogous estimate from weighted component GI values; no direct GI measurement of this composed dish has been published. GL per 400g bowl ~39.5 is classified very high and represents a significant health-halo risk for Type 2 Diabetes patients. 8. Vegan status: the dish as described contains honey, which is not vegan by strict definition; dietary_flags.is_vegan is set to false accordingly. 9. Monounsaturated fat (~1.2g/100g) and polyunsaturated fat (~0.9g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles of açaí purée and peanut butter components; these are approximate. 10. FoodEx2 code A0SMB used as closest parent (smoothie/blended fruit bowl); no exact FoodEx2 code exists for açaí bowl. 11. Aliases self-check performed (q8): every string value in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array; all entries confirmed consistent.
